entry.title: Re: Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - adding S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
@christiaens I've made a version of the Basic table with a tap event enabled. You can copy-paste this token to the Marketplace search to get the component to your app:



Note that when you do the navigation after the "Component rowTap" event takes place, `self.tappedItem.id` for example will give you an error in the formula editor because the output value is of type Any value (since the fields of the entity that is connected to the table are dynamic). But it will work 🙂

Example config (when I know that "name" field exists in the entity):

 



How I created it:

  1. Select the Basic table
  2. Go to Component Template editor
  3. Switch over to Properties > Output values
  4. Create an output value "tappedItem" of type "Any value" AND an event "rowTap"
  5. Find all the containers in the component that should trigger a change to the output value and the "rowTap" event ("Row: Odd" and "Row: "Even")
  6. Attach to the Component Tap events of these two containers "Set output value" flow function, where you set "tappedItem" to "Data item in repeat" -> "Row" and trigger the "rowTap"
The component now has an output value that holds the object that has been tapped and an event that gets triggered when the row is tapped.

entry.title: Criteria for copying journal entries
entry.content:
Dear Community,

we have noticed that it is possible to copy some journal entries in app 'Manage Journal Entries', but not others. It is not entirely clear which requirements must be met for a posting document to be copied. We believe that we identified some criteria, for example that reverse documents can not be copied. But we still have some cases where it is not clear to us why copying the document is not possible. 

Can anyone help out with a list of criteria that needs to be met, so that a journal entry can be copied in app 'Manage Journal Entries'?

Thanks in advance!

Regards,
Niklas

entry.title: An Overview for Warehouse Order in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi All 

In this blog I will discuss an overview for warehouse Order

Introduction- 

Document that represents an executable work package that a warehouse employee should perform at a specific time. The warehouse order consists of warehouse tasks or physical inventory items. When we move products, EWM create warehouse tasks, then EWM groups these warehouse tasks together into warehouse orders, and makes them available for processing. When we perform a physical inventory, we create physical inventory items, EWM also groups these physical inventory items together into warehouse orders. We can also use warehouse order together with wave management. We can also use warehouse orders together with wave management. Waves are used to group warehouse requests to allow the creation of multiple warehouse tasks at the same time. We can schedule the release of waves for task creation to enable efficient task management in the warehouse. These tasks can then be grouped into warehouse orders for further assignment to resources.

Configuring steps for Warehouse Orders

The configuration settings required for filters, limits, packing profiles, and the like. These settings help set up an executable work package for the warehouse resource. The resource can complete multiple open tasks in an activity area together, thus reducing travel into and out of the warehouse for each task execution.

Define Sort rules for warehouse task

Use this setting to define the sort rules and assign them to one or more sort fields. Click on New Entries, and, for each sort rule, define the fields in the warehouse tasks that can be used for sorting and whether they need to be sorted in ascending or descending order.

IMG  • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Sort Rules for Warehouse Tasks

Filters for warehouse order creation rule

Filters are used to set rules for processing a warehouse task. Embedded EWM  Warehouse Order applies filters on warehouse tasks that have not been assigned to any warehouse order to check if they can be processed by a warehouse order creation rule.

IMG-Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Filters for Warehouse Order Creation Rules

There are two types of filters that can be used in warehouse order creation based on the value set in Filter Type

Item filter

Item filters are used to check individual warehouse tasks for a warehouse order and are defined by setting Filter Type to I Filter at Item Level. The system checks to see if the warehouse task is within the min/max volume, weight, and processing limits of the item filter. It also checks whether the warehouse task has been created using a specific warehouse process type, route, wave category, and reason for movement. If a warehouse task doesn’t meet the filtering criteria, system checks to see if it can use the next warehouse order creation rule in the sequence to process the warehouse task.

Subtotal filter

Embedded EWM uses subtotal filters to compare several warehouse tasks against a subtotal; they’re defined by setting Filter Type to S Filter at Subtotal Level. To use subtotal filters, it’s important to sort warehouse tasks using sorting rules with a sort field consolidation group.

Limit value for size of warehouse order

The limit value of a warehouse order creation rule specifies the size of a warehouse order. The limit value is assigned to each warehouse order creation rule. Warehouse tasks assigned to a warehouse order creation rule are assessed for limit values, which are used to set a limit on the size of the warehouse order. As soon as the warehouse task exceeds any of the limit filter criteria, it’s taken out of the existing warehouse order and assigned to a new warehouse order using the same warehouse order creation rule. The system again applies a limit filter to the warehouse tasks assigned to the new warehouse order. A limit filter can be used to set limits for maximum/ minimum warehouse tasks in a warehouse order, maximum/minimum weight, volume, processing time, and so on

IMG-Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Limit Values for the Size of a Warehouse Order

Note - You can set a limit value type (Limit Val. Type) of Physical Inventory if warehouse tasks included in the warehouse order are for physical inventory

Packing profile

 Packing profiles are used to create pick HUs during the creation of warehouse orders for outbound/picking processes. Using a packing profile, the system determines a packaging specification and HU type using the packaging material master data for the pick HU. The system uses data such as weight, volume, capacity, and so on from the assigned warehouse order when determining the appropriate number of pick HUs in the embedded EWM system. For example, if the assigned warehouse tasks don’t fit into one HU, then the system creates another pick HU using a predefined packaging material

IMG -Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Packing Profile for Warehouse Order Creation

Note- There are multiple parameters that control the creation of pick Hus

Pack Mode

Sorting

Create HU

Assign Warehouse Task to HU

Split Warehouse Task

Split Warehouse Task Based on AUoM

SKIP Warehouse TasK

Check LWH

Warehouse order creation rule

The filters, profiles, and limits described previously are used to create a container in the form of a warehouse order creation rule that creates warehouse orders for open warehouse tasks. The warehouse  order creation rule also contains the time required for the worker to complete the warehouse order

IMG- Extended Warehouse Management • Cross Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Creation Rules for Warehouse Order

You can review the created warehouse order creation rules.

IMG-Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Overview of Creation Rules for Warehouse Orders

Determination of warehouse order creation rule

Warehouse orders are used to group multiple warehouse tasks in a single work package. To determine which warehouse order creation rule is applicable for a warehouse task, a search sequence for the warehouse order creation rule can be assigned to a combination of activity area and activity.

IMG-• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Search Sequence of Creation Rules for Activity Areas

Note - You can define more than one warehouse order creation rule for an activity area and activity. This way, the system searches for a warehouse order creation rule based on the defined search sequence and picks the first warehouse order creation rule that fits the required filtering criteria. For warehouse order creation rule determination, it’s mandatory to sort the storage bins for activity area and activity.

Manual Assembly of Warehouse Orders

This function is used in the goods receipt process to delete warehouse tasks of different HUs from an existing warehouse order and group them in a new warehouse order using both SAP GUI and RF. This function is optimized so that you can operate it using a hand-held scanner that is installed on a keyboard filter. You can specify multiple HUs in the entry field, one after each other. EWM shows you the specified HU in the lower part of the screen, and assigns the HU to a position field, ascending. For example, you scan three HUs. EWM assigns the HU scanned first to position one, the next HU to position two, and the HU scanned last to position three. Make sure that the position of the HU in EWM matches the physical position of the HU in the resource. When you perform putaway, EWM shows you the position in the Radio Frequency dialog .

Green- you have specified a valid HU, and that warehouse tasks exist for all products in the HU. EWM has updated screen content for the total weight and total volume, and increased the number of HUs in the new warehouse order that is to be created by one.

Red-you have specified an HU, but that no warehouse task exists for at least one of the products in the HU. EWM does not update the screen content for the total weight and total volume, and does not include the HU in the new warehouse order that is to be created.

Printing Warehouse Orders

EWM allows automatic printing of warehouse orders during their creation using smart forms or SAPscript. The warehouse order printing activity also can be carried out from the warehouse monitor. The printing is carried out via the PPF, along with the use of a condition technique. For customizing go in below path.

IMG-Extended Warehouse Management • Warehouse Order • Print • Assign Determination Procedure.

  • EWM automatically triggers the print run from processing, or you trigger it manually
  • EWM determines the print determination procedure assigned to the warehouse process type
  • PPF calls the /SCWM/WME application with action profile /SCWM/WO and checks for the condition record for the print action definition
  • On successfully finding both the schedule and trigger condition, the system triggers the function module to print the SAP Smart Form.
  • The PPF saves the action executed and issues print logs

Some Action Definitions and Smart Forms

Note- If there are no more warehouse order creation rules left in the sequence for a warehouse order, the system assigns them to default rule UNDE.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - adding S/4HANA Cloud

Hi @christiaens I created a version of the table where the tap event works, you can get it by pasting this token to the Marketplace search in your app:



Here's how to configure it (the `self.tappedItem.propertyName` formula will throw an error but as long as you use a property that you know exists, it should be all good):

The more detailed steps are:

  1. Select the Basic table
  2. Go to Component Template editor
  3. Switch over to Properties > Output values
  4. Create an output value "tappedItem" of type "Any value" AND an event "rowTap"
  5. Find all the containers in the component that should trigger a change to the output value and the "rowTap" event ("Row: Odd" and "Row: "Even")
  6. Attach to the Component Tap events of these two containers "Set output value" flow function, where you set "tappedItem" to "Data item in repeat" -> "Row" and trigger the "rowTap"
The component now has an output value that holds the object that has been tapped and an event that gets triggered when the row is tapped.
 
Let me know if this helps!

entry.title: Re: cannot create new report in LMS
entry.content: In your preview instance (you cannot do this in production), you'll be able to to download it from the report tab, if you have the Reports Developer role. We have to have SAP add the role every time we refresh our preview instance, so if it's not available in yours. you may need to open a ticket. Once you have the proper access, you should see Learning Report Designer on the Report tab of the reports page. Once you click there, it will give you the download and all the information to be able to connect to your preview instance. There is some training out there on the PRD tool and you will also need the Data Dictionary with all the tables and field if you want to add additional information to any report.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Passing FREE SELECTIONS in SUBMIT Without VIA SELECTION-SCREEN
entry.content: In my case, ZTEST017 is a copy of the standard program RFBLIA00 (used by F.01 transaction). This program utilizes a logical database, and there's a specific standard function module within RFBLIA00 that appears to reset my custom values for FREE SELECTIONS (e.g., PRCTR) and replaces them with default or system-determined values.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Passing FREE SELECTIONS in SUBMIT Without VIA SELECTION-SCREEN
entry.content:
What I’ve Done:

  • To address this, I copied RFBLIA00 into a custom program (ZTEST017) to gain control over this behavior.
  • I ensured that the logical database setup remains intact for the custom report.
  • My custom implementation allows me to use FREE SELECTIONS, but I’m encountering this issue only when executing the program without VIA SELECTION-SCREEN in the SUBMIT statement.

The Issue:

The clearing and replacement of my custom FREE SELECTIONS values occur due to the logic in the copied program or its associated logical database routines. This behavior happens even though the FREE SELECTIONS expressions (texpr) are populated correctly in my program.

entry.title: Release of SAP Disclosure Management Stack 2200
entry.content:
Discover the new DM Stack 2200: More sustainability, more flexibility! 

We are pleased to announce the release of DM Stack 2200 of our software! This latest version brings numerous improvements and new features to make your work more efficient and productive. From enhanced XBRL functionalities to new data connections – Stack 2200 has something for everyone. Read on to discover all the highlights! 

 

Create sustainability reports with SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT)

In a world where sustainability is becoming increasingly important, it is crucial for companies to transparently and understandably present their progress in this area. SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) offers a comprehensive solution for collecting, analyzing, and reporting sustainability data. With the new version of SAP Disclosure Management, you can access and process consolidated data from the SAP SCT to create meaningful sustainability reports.  

 

New XBRL extensions for ESEF and CSRS reports 

The European Single Electronic Format (ESEF) and the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Standards (CSRS) have made significant progress in recent years to improve the transparency and comparability of financial and sustainability information. A central component of these developments is the use of XBRL (eXtensible Business Reporting Language), a standardized language for representing business reports in electronic form. To further enhance the efficiency and accuracy of these reports, new XBRL extensions have been developed, which we will explore below. 

Support for Enumerations 

One of the most important innovations is enumerations. The new taxonomies can contain these, offering a predefined list of values that can be used for specific concepts in the reports. This increases the consistency and uniformity of the reports, as companies no longer need to define their own values. This reduces the risk of errors and misunderstandings and facilitates the analysis and comparison of data. More information on enumerations in our SAP Help.   

Support for Typed Dimensions 

Typed Dimensions offer a more precise and flexible way to represent multidimensional data in XBRL reports. Unlike conventional dimensions, which often have flat structures, Typed Dimensions allow the definition of complex hierarchies and relationships. This is particularly important for ESEF and CSRS reports, which often contain extensive and detailed information on financial and sustainability performance. More information on Typed Dimensions in our SAP Help. 

Single Mode for Mappings 

Another significant advancement is the Single Mode for Mappings. This allows individual concepts to be edited without changing other concepts. This granularity increases the flexibility and precision in creating reports. Companies can now make specific adjustments to ensure that their data is accurately represented and contains all relevant information. 

New Calculation and Validation Options

Additionally, new validation options have been introduced to further improve the quality and accuracy of the reports. These options allow companies to check their data against a variety of rules and standards before submission. This ensures that the reports meet all necessary requirements and contain no significant errors. More information on the new options in our SAP Help. 

Summarized Preview for a Concepts 

In ESEF and CSRS reporting, the correct representation and mapping of text concepts play a crucial role. The ability to obtain a partial preview for individual text concepts offers several advantages: 

 - Companies can ensure that each text concept is presented exactly as expected by the auditors. This reduces the risk of errors and misunderstandings. 

- The partial preview allows quick and easy verification of the correctness of the distributed mapped areas. This saves time and resources in creating and reviewing the reports. 

How does the partial preview work?

The new feature is intuitive and easy to use. When you select a text-based concept in your report, you now have the option to generate a partial preview in the right information pane. This preview shows you how the distributed mapped areas are summarized – exactly as the auditors will see them. 

 

New CSV reports for EBA

**What are the new CSV reports?** 

The EBA has introduced new reporting formats in CSV format to promote standardization and automation of data transmission. This is supported with this version through a new plugin.  

 

New feature in our Batch Client: Downloads now possible! 

Our Batch Client has received a long-awaited feature – downloads! From now on, you cannot only upload files in batches but also download them directly. More information on the new options in our SAP Help. 

Previously, you could efficiently upload files with our Batch Client, but many of you asked for a way to download the processed files as well.

entry.title: Delete items (BSEG) from a parked FI document with FM PRELIMINARY_POSTING_DOC_WRITE
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm struggling with the function module PRELIMINARY_POSTING_DOC_WRITE, when I try to delete lines from VBSEG. I have to do so because I need to apply changes to the parked document coming from an interface outside of SAP where I cannot determine the needed changes on field level. Or: Let's say it can happen that the existing document has one vendor and 2 GL lines (split posting on different GL accounts) but now only one GL account line is needed or vice versa.

My approach is the following: I first read the document with FM PRELIMINARY_POSTING_DOC_READ. Then I would delete all existing lines and create all lines needed after the update from the beginning. For the deletion of the existing lines I tried so set VBSEG-KZ to 'D' and set VBSEG-BZALT to the old VBSEG-BUZEI value. For the insert I tried to set VBSEG-KZ to 'I' and set BUZEI to new values starting with the highest old VBSEG-BUZEI + 1 value. Unfortunately that did not work. I also tried other approaches using the import parameter i_bstat = 'U' or setting the value (WRBTR) in the lines to be deleted to zero. Either I got dumps with duplicate key errors or the old lines have not been deleted and the new lines have been added to the document (occasionally with redundant BUZEI values).

Does anyone have a solution for this?

Regards, Oliver

entry.title: CAP: Time-traveling on CDS level in views with temporal aspect not possible anymore in CDS 8
entry.content:
Hi,

In my CAP datamodel/services I was able to 'time-travel' entities and views with the temporal aspect, using annotations: capire reference.

An example of a view:

...selecting from

 

We upgraded to CDS 8, and now we notice only 'valid' records are queried, even though we use the annotations to time-travel or suppress the where query.

Another thing we notice is that in the Database Explorer, all views with temporal aspect display 0 records. Even if this view would be a simple projection on an entity with temporal aspect.

So it looks like either the CAP framework generates these DB artefacts differently in the HDB now in CDS 8, or the framework handles the queries differently and does not apply the suppression of the temporal aspect.

I know time-traveling queries is possible using url parameters, but that's not an option here. 

Is there anyone who can help?

entry.title: SAP API Management - OAuth 2.0 authentication with SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

I need some help in understanding the SAML Assertion based OAuth authentication set up between SAP API Management (SAP APIM) and SuccessFactors (SF) system. I followed this blog post to set this up.

With an exception that I used "https://mySuccessFactorsTenant.com/oauth/token" end point to retrieve the OAuth token instead of using "https://mySuccessFactorsTenant.com/oauth/idp" end point, since this will be deprecated.

The idea is to not to expose the MyHr system directly to the open internet and to have security measures(OAuth 2.0 and TLS 1.2) in place.

The Proxy with this set up is working fine and we are able to connect to back-end SF system and do all operations. I have the following questions:

  1. How can I check the TLS version being used. I came across this post which says that TLS 1.2 is enforced by default. Is this also true for connection between the Consumer of the API Proxy and SAP APIM?
  2. Since the SAML assertion for OAuth with SF is being generated by a policy inside SAP APIM proxy here, can it be said that SAP APIM is idp here?
  3. New Requirements:
    1. SF wants to create dedicated Users for each Consumer to restrict access/authorizations - For now, I have implemented the logic to get the Username during runtime based on Application name and then use that Username to create Assertion/token so that authentication is done with User assigned for calling Consumer. Is this the correct way to achieve this? Any alternative way to do this?
    2. We are also required to use OAuth 2.0 for all requests coming to SAP APIM. I understand that one way to achieve this would be to have a service in SAP APIM which the consumer can call to get the OAuth token (Client credential based OAuth) as described here. This approach will result in 2 OAuth set-ups, one between Consumer <--> SAP APIM and another one between SAP APIM <--> SF.

Considering requirement from Point 3.1 and 3.2 above, what would be the recommended approach for the overall Integration i.e. to have OAuth 2.0 set up for end to end communication? Please advise.

Regards,

Faisal

entry.title: Best Practices for Running Cron Jobs and Business Processes in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
The cronjob functionality is used for executing tasks, called cron jobs, regularly at a certain point of time. Typically cron jobs can be used for creating data for backups, updating catalog contents, or recalculating prices. The key idea of applying cron jobs is to start a long or periodic process in the background, with the possibility to log each run and to easily check its result. The concept of cron jobs in SAP Commerce Cloud is explained in detail here.

In this article, we will go through some best practices for running CronJobs and business processes in SAP Commerce Cloud

Writing an Abortable Job:

Custom Jobs are not abortable by default, which means it won't be possible to stop them if they are running for too long.
Because job execution may take a lot of time, you may wish to abort a job. To do this, add a code snippet for checking if you wish to abort a job. Because you can not easily and safely abort a JobPerformable at any time unless you kill the thread, it is necessary to place hooks for the abort before stages that you expect to be time-consuming. See How to write an abortable job.

  • Identifying if a Cronjob is abortable:
    CronJobs come with a method called isAbortable that can be used to test this.
    To test it, its possible to use the following Groovy script, changing the value of 'Your-CronJob' to the actual CronJob code.

    def cronjob = defaultCronJobService.getCronJob('Your-Cronjob'); //change to your cronjob code
    if (defaultCronJobService.isAbortable(cronjob))
    println cronjob.getCode() + " is Abortable"
    else
    println cronjob.getCode() + " is not Abortable"

    3209129 - How to Test if a Cronjob is Abortable - SAP Commerce
    2728389 - How to abort a CronJob

Assigning a Cron Job to a Group of Nodes:

You can assign a cron job to a group of nodes. It can then be executed by any of the nodes belonging to that group. If a node that is currently executing a cron job stops operating, one of the other nodes from that group takes over and executes it.
Avoid running cron jobs on frontend nodes, such as accstorefront and API nodes, as these are primarily dedicated to maintaining website stability. Running cron jobs on accstorefront nodes, for example, can result in high CPU usage, memory issues, and other performance-related problems. Similar issues can occur if cron jobs are run on API pods.

  • Use Dedicated Background Nodes:
    Use dedicated background nodes to run your cron jobs and jobs. Specifically, run the cron jobs in the backgroundprocessing and backoffice nodes. For catalog synchronization jobs, it is recommended to run them on the backgroundprocessing nodes. See Assigning a Cron Job to a Group of Nodes

Avoid Running Multiple Resource-Intensive Jobs Simultaneously:

When dealing with resource-intensive cron jobs or business processes, it’s important to avoid running them simultaneously, as this can quickly exhaust system resources. The simultaneous execution of multiple heavy jobs can lead to performance issues, out-of-memory errors, high DTU consumption, high CPU utilization etc. all of which can compromise the stability of the application.

To minimize these risks, schedule jobs with intervals to spread out resource-heavy tasks. For example, if you have multiple cron jobs that need to run regularly, schedule them using triggers at different times to prevent peak load on the system. See Trigger.

If you run heavy job on backoffice node, it could affect the performance of users browsing the backoffice, the same way it can affect storefront users.

Running CronJobs in Sequence:

If you have multiple jobs that need to run sequentially, you can set up a Composite CronJob, to run multtiple jobs one after the other. See Running CronJobs in Sequence

Data Maintenance and Cleanup:

This section covers the technical data that the platform accumulates over time and how to properly clean it up.

The platform ships with the cleanup capabilities. However, some of these capabilities require additional configuration to enable. The usual areas of consideration for this category are:

  • Cronjobs
  • Cronjob Logs
  • Cronjob History
  • ImpEx Media
  • Saved Values
  • Stored HTTP Sessions
  • Workflows
  • Distributed ImpEx
  • Media Deletions

All sample configuration in this section is provided on best-effort basis. Make sure to verify and to adapt it to your project!

Cronjobs:

Over time, many cronjobs instances will accumulate in your SAP Commerce Cloud database

The most frequent jobs are:

  • ImpEx Imports/Exports
  • Catalog Synchronization
  • Solr Jobs

To clean those up, you can easily configure a retention job with an ImpEx script like the following:

$twoWeeks = 1209600

INSERT_UPDATE FlexibleSearchRetentionRule;code[unique=true];searchQuery;retentionTimeSeconds;actionReference;
; cronjobCleanupRule;"select {c:pk}, {c:itemType}
 from {CronJob as c join ComposedType as t on {c:itemtype} = {t:pk} left join Trigger as trg on {trg:cronjob} = {c:pk} }
 where
   {trg:pk} is null and
   {c:code} like '00______%' and
   {t:code} in ( 'ImpExImportCronJob', 'CatalogVersionSyncCronJob', 'SolrIndexerCronJob' ) and
   {c:endTime} < ?CALC_RETIREMENT_TIME"; $twoWeeks; basicRemoveCleanupAction;

INSERT_UPDATE RetentionJob;code[unique=true];retentionRule(code);batchSize
; cronjobRetentionJob; cronjobCleanupRule; 1000

INSERT_UPDATE CronJob;code[unique=true];job(code);sessionLanguage(isoCode)[default=en]
; cronjobRetentionCronJob; cronjobRetentionJob;

INSERT_UPDATE Trigger; cronJob(code)[unique = true]   ; cronExpression
; cronjobRetentionCronJob ; 0 0 0 * * ?

A few notes regarding the above configuration:

  • It aggressively cleans up all jobs older than two weeks, regardless of the cronjob result
  • The code 'where' clause restricts it to auto-generated jobs
  • It only targets unscheduled cronjobs (= cronjobs without a trigger)
  • Cleaning up is done once per day, at midnight

The good part about using retention rules is that they are easily configurable, as shown in the example above.

Note: An alternative way for cleaning up cronjobs would be the CleanupCronJobStrategy for the legacy Maintenance Framework. However, that strategy requires customization if you want to change which cronjobs it processes.

Cronjob Logs:

To actually clean up old cronjob log files as described in  CronJob Logs Clean-up, ensure that you configure a cronjob and trigger to delete the logs.

The platform does not clean up old log files out-of-the-box!

The following is a sample ImpEx script which can be used to generate and run a cleanup job:

INSERT_UPDATE CronJob;code[unique=true];job(code);sessionLanguage(isoCode)[default=en]
; cronjobLogCleanupCronjob; cleanUpLogsJobPerformable;

INSERT_UPDATE Trigger; cronJob(code)[unique = true];cronExpression
# every hour
; cronjobLogCleanupCronjob ; 0 0 0/1 * * ?

If you have cronjobs that run very frequently (for example, every few minutes), you should schedule the log file cleanup even more frequently. Running the cleanup more frequently avoids building up too many log files that need to be deleted.

Cronjob Histories:

SAP Commerce Cloud uses Cronjob Histories to track the progress of cronjobs. Similar to cronjob logs, they can accumulate quickly for frequently running jobs.

Starting with SAP Commerce 2005, the platform includes a cleanup cronjob for Cronjob Histories (documentation).

In case you are on an older patch release and cannot upgrade, please refer to the SAP Knowledge Base Note 2848601 for the impex file that sets up the cleanup job.

Cleaning up Cronjob histories is critically important for the performance.

Make sure that the Cronjob histories cleanup job is enabled and active!

ImpexMedia:

Every ImpEx import or export generates at least one ImpexMedia. These media stay in the system, the platform does not delete them when it deletes the ImpEx jobs they belonged to (ImpEx media can potentially be re-used for other ImpEx jobs, but that's rarely ever the case). To set up a retention job for Media, use the following sample ImpEx script:

$twoWeeks = 1209600
INSERT_UPDATE FlexibleSearchRetentionRule;code[unique=true];searchQuery;retentionTimeSeconds;actionReference;
;impexMediaCleanupRule;"select {i:pk}, {i:itemtype}
   from {ImpexMedia as i}
   where
   {i:code} like '00______' and
   {i:modifiedTime} < ?CALC_RETIREMENT_TIME"; $twoWeeks; basicRemoveCleanupAction;

INSERT_UPDATE RetentionJob;code[unique=true];retentionRule(code);batchSize
; impexMediaCleanupJob; impexMediaCleanupRule; 1000

INSERT_UPDATE CronJob;code[unique=true];job(code);sessionLanguage(isoCode)[default=en]
; impexMediaCleanupCronJob; impexMediaCleanupJob;

INSERT_UPDATE Trigger; cronJob(code)[unique = true]   ; cronExpression
# every day at midnight
; impexMediaCleanupCronJob ; 0 0 0 * * ?

The retention time should be the same as for the cronjob cleanup.

entry.title: Handling mandatory fields in a multi-country environment in SuccessFactors Recruiting
entry.content:
Problem Statement

Consider a scenario wherein a Recruiter has to complete certain Screening details on the candidate application and these details have to be completed in stages, i.e., say for example, starting from New Application up until Interview statuses he/she has the option to complete them.

Please note, these fields are not mandatory up until the Offer status, however, beyond this stage they have to be filled in compulsorily and cannot be left blank any longer.

As this stage, in SuccessFactors Recruiting we do not have an option to mark these fields as mandatory, yet we have to ensure that the Recruiter completes these fields before proceeding any further.

Resolution

To handle this issue, we will have to create a picklist field in the candidate application with picklist value "Yes" only. This picklist field will have to be mandatory as shown in the screenshot below :

Now, list down all the mandatory screening fields under one dedicated section as shown below :

Business Rule Logic

A business rule has to be written which checks if each and every field under the Mandatory section shown above is filled or not. If any one of the fields is blank, the rule has to reset the value of the picklist field shown in the first screenshot to Null.

Business Rule Assignation

The business rule has to be assigned under Job Application section and OnChange of each of these fields under the mandatory section, including the mandatory picklist field itself, from screenshot 1.

Sample Rule

Conclusion

The above rule will help ensure that all the mandatory fields are being filled though they do not have an asterisk against them indicating that they are mandatory fields per se.

entry.title: Re: SCIM API Filter is throwing an error when using SAP S...
entry.content:
Together with SAP support, I have now found out that this isn't supported as of today (see here).

Fully qualified names (<schema>:<attribute>) are not supported.

  • For example: GET .../Users/urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User:employee Number eq '<attribute>'

So you can only query individual attributes in the context of Proxy <.<

  • userName
  • displayname
  • userType
  • emails
  • etc..

entry.title: Fiori elements preview not displaying cds view fields
entry.content:
Hi everyone

I have a cds view that extracts some data from the swwflexproc, swwwihead and swwuserwi tables. I have already created the necessary service definition and binding for the view. However after activating and publishing the service binding, when I run the application using the fiori elements preview, the fields that display do not match up with those defined in my cds view. I am new to abap and followed the following tutorial: Create an ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) View in ABAP On-Premise | SAP Tutorials , https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-create-cds-view.html  but cannot seem to figure out where I am going wrong. Any assistance would be greatly appreciated.

my cds view definition:

define view Z_Test_Report with parameters @Environment.systemField: #SYSTEM_DATE p_currdate: datum

as select from swwwihead as ihead

left outer join swwuserwi as userwi on ihead.wi_id = userwi.wi_id

left outer join swwflexproc as flex on ihead.wi_id = flex.wi_id

{

key flex.appl_obj_id as AppplicationObjectID,

ihead.wi_id as WorkItemID,

ihead.wi_cd as Date,

ihead.wi_aagent as Agent,

coalesce(ihead.wi_stat, 'In progress') as Status,

dats_days_between(ihead.wi_cd, :p_currdate) as Overdue,

ihead.wi_text as Comments,

}

Fields for selection in the fiori elements preview:

service definition:

@EndUserText.label: 'srvc definition exposing Z_Test_Report'

define service Z_CDS_DEFINITION {

expose Z_Test_Report as TR;

}

entry.title: Re: System view not showing data in Container
entry.content:
Most of system views have some logic to filter the data depending on user system privileges. As you can see in the screenshot, M_TABLE_STATISTICS will return no result if you don't have system privilege CATALOG READ or DATA ADMIN.

When running a data preview in BAS or WebIDE for HANA, the query will execute with the application user  of your hdi container (user ..._RT). To grant necessary privilege to application user, you should use .hdbgrants artifact (see https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4505d0bdaf4948449b7f7379d24d0f0d/f49c1f5c72ee453788bf79f113d83bf9.html )

entry.title: Re: How to connect salesforce to datasphere ?
entry.content:
Hi, integrating Salesforce with SAP Datasphere can greatly improve data accessibility and optimize workflows. For a successful Salesforce SAP integration, you’ll typically need to:

  1. Set up a secure connection using SAP’s Cloud Connector, which involves installing and configuring it to link your SAP environment with the cloud.
  2. Configure OAuth authentication in Salesforce, including creating a connected app and setting appropriate permissions.
  3. Define data models in SAP Datasphere to align with Salesforce objects for seamless data flow.

If you have specific use cases—such as syncing customer data or reports—I’d be happy to share more targeted steps or direct you to detailed resources. Let me know what your priorities are for this integration.

It is only possible to copy manually created Journal entries posted through the 'Post General Journal Entries' app. You will not be able to use the 'Copy' function in this app to copy the journal entries that were auto created based on sub ledger postings (MM/AP/AR/AA). 

Please refer to the comments section of this Customer Influence Portal (CIP) request:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/278776/?section=sectionDetails

Shreya

entry.title: Exploring Your SAP Solution: Engaging Stakeholders Early in the Implementation Process
entry.content:
Why is it important to familiarize yourself with your solution prior to implementation?

Let me share a story about a customer I worked with who took the time to enable their team before starting the project implementation and design discussions.

This customer was new to SAP and purchased a solution that was unfamiliar to them, yet critical for their business. As we recommend to all our customers, we advised them to undergo solution enablement before project implementation. This approach would help them become more familiar with the solution and maximize its value throughout the implementation process.

You’re probably wondering how it went for that customer. Well, I can tell you—it went really well!

When we began the design workshops, we presented the standard capabilities, as we always recommend to our customers for creating a scalable solution. However, we also recognize that business-specific requirements often call for adjustments. During these discussions, our customer was not only better prepared to engage with the topics and explanations we provided, but they were also able to identify key functionalities that would help enhance their business.

These design discussions are crucial during project implementation because they define the foundation of the customer’s solution. If the design is not done correctly from the start, the customer may end up with a solution that isn’t viable. Moreover, having the right solution design in place ensures that, during the testing phase, you're validating the solution effectively—rather than testing irrelevant processes that don’t align with the business needs. And that’s exactly what happened—my customer ended up with a solution that perfectly met their needs.

Another benefit was the time efficiency. By enabling themselves early on, the workshop discussions ran smoothly and stayed within the defined timelines, allowing us to progress without any delays. And it wasn’t just in the early stages where we were time-efficient; throughout all phases of the project implementation, we stayed on track because the scope of what needed to be delivered was clear from the start.

Finally, the entire team was engaged from day one and understood the impact of implementing a new solution. Typically, when a new solution is being implemented, not all users immediately embrace the change. Senior leadership is eager to move to a new system to improve business performance, increase margins and save costs, but business users are often resistant, especially when they are unfamiliar with the new system.

However, by enabling key stakeholders early on, we were able to make a significant impact on the skeptical business users. These stakeholders were able to champion the solution, sharing the added value it brought for the business users by streamlining their daily tasks. This empowered the business users to engage during the discussions and focus on higher-value activities, keeping them motivate. It also reinforced that everyone was contributing to the overall success of the business.

In a nutshell:

Gaining knowledge about SAP solution ahead of time is crucial, allows customers to maximize the value of the system from day one. 

By familiarizing themselves with the features, functionalities, and best practices before implementation, customers can ensure a smoother transition, reduce potential challenges, and align the solution more effectively with their business goals. This proactive approach allows teams to ask informed questions, make strategic decisions, and prepare their organization for a more seamless integration.

Furthermore, early exposure to the SAP solution empowers customers to take full advantage of its capabilities, ensuring they are ready to leverage its tools and insights to drive business success once the system goes live.

Stay curious and never stop learning!

 

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Ana Barreira

    Serves as the Global Onboarding Advisor for the SAP Transportation Management (TM) solution within the Supply Chain Management area. She provides expert guidance and support to new SAP customers from contract signature through to successful go-live. With eleven years of experience—three of which have been as a Global Onboarding Advisor, and eight years as an SAP Consultant—Ana specializes in the SAP TM solution. She has successfully supported full-cycle customer implementation projects, assisted partners, and contributed to internal enablement efforts

    Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @Ana_Barreira or @Linh-Hoang .

    ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: SAP Incentive Management - Understanding of CSN file & CLI Command to generate
entry.content:
Understanding Schema Notation (CSN) and Its Role in Embedded Analytics for SAP Incentive Management

Introduction to Schema Notation (CSN)

Schema Notation (CSN) is a powerful and flexible format used to represent the structure and metadata of data models in SAP's Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model. CSN files are JSON-based and provide a comprehensive way to describe entities, relationships, and other aspects of a data model. This notation is particularly useful for reporting developers working with SAP IM (Incentive Management) solutions, as it facilitates the integration and analysis of complex data structures.

Why CSN is Important

CSN files serve as a bridge between the design-time and runtime environments. They allow developers to define their data models in a human-readable format, which can then be easily transformed into various runtime artifacts. This capability is crucial for embedded analytics, where real-time data insights are necessary for making informed business decisions.

Benefits of Using CSN for Embedded Analytics (SAC)

  1. Consistency and Accuracy: CSN ensures that the data model definitions are consistent across different environments. This consistency is vital for accurate data analysis and reporting.

  2. Ease of Integration: CSN files can be easily integrated with various SAP tools and services, making it simpler to embed analytics into SAP Incentive Management solutions. This integration allows for seamless data flow and real-time insights.

  3. Flexibility: The JSON-based structure of CSN files makes them highly flexible and easy to manipulate. Developers can quickly adjust the data models to meet changing business requirements without extensive rework.

  4. Enhanced Collaboration: CSN files are human-readable, which facilitates better collaboration among development teams. Everyone involved in the project can understand and contribute to the data model definitions.

Generating CSN Files

To generate a CSN file from a CDS (Core Data Services) model, you can use the cds command-line tool. Here’s a step-by-step guide:

  1. Install the CDS Development Kit: Ensure you have the CDS development kit installed globally on your system:

    npm install -g @sap/cds-dk

  2. Navigate to Your Project Directory: Open your terminal and navigate to the directory containing your CDS files.

  3. Compile the CDS Model to CSN: Use the following command to compile your CDS model into a CSN file:

    cds compile <your-cds-file>.cds --to csn --dest <output-file>.csn

    Replace <your-cds-file>.cds with the name of your CDS file and <output-file>.csn with the desired name for the output CSN file.

Detailed Instructions

https://github.com/yogananda-muthaiah/SAP-Sucessfactors-Incentive-Management/blob/main/Integrations/EA/csn.md 

Practical Example

Consider a scenario where you need to analyze sales data within SAP Incentive Management. By defining your custom data model in CDS and generating a CSN file, you can consume analytics directly into your EA solution. This approach allows sales teams to access real-time insights, such as sales trends and customer preferences, directly within the EA interface.

Conclusion

Schema Notation (CSN) plays a crucial role in the development and deployment of embedded analytics within SAP Incentive Management integrated solutions. By providing a consistent, flexible, and easy-to-integrate format for data models, CSN enables developers to deliver powerful analytics capabilities that drive informed decision-making and enhance business performance.

entry.title: column width alignment missing for drop down values in SAP UI5 application.
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing column width issue in my SAP UI5 application. For Enter Code column, if there are no dropdown values it should be disabled and if there are drop down values it should display the drop down values. Please let me know how to correct the column alignment.

Below is my code in my view.xml file. 

                <IconTabFilter id="iconTabBarTakeReading" text="{i18n>TakeReading}" icon="sap-icon://measurement-document" count="{detailView>/CountMeasuringData}">
                                <Table id="bindMeasuringPoint" noDataText="{i18n>lblNoMeasuringData}" alternateRowColors="true" headerText="{i18n>ttlMeasuringData}"
                                       class="sapUiResponsiveMargin" autoPopinMode="true" >
                                    <columns>
                                        <Column demandPopin="true" hAlign="Begin">
                                            <Text text="{i18n>lblMeasuringPoint}"/>
                                        </Column>
                                        <Column demandPopin="true" hAlign="Begin">
                                            <Text text="{i18n>lblMeasuringDescription}"/>
                                        </Column>
                                        <Column demandPopin="true" hAlign="Begin">
                                            <Text text="{i18n>lblEnterReading}"/>                                            
                                        </Column>
                                        <Column id="column50">
                                            <header>
                                                <Text text="{i18n>lblValuationCode}"/>
                                            </header>
                                        </Column>
                                        <Column demandPopin="true" hAlign="Begin" width="7rem">
                                            <Text text="{i18n>lblUoM}"/>
                                        </Column>
                                        <Column demandPopin="true" hAlign="Begin">
                                            <Text text="{i18n>lblLastReading}"/>
                                        </Column>
                                        <Column id="column51">
                                            <header>
                                                <Text text="{i18n>lblPreviousValuationCode}"/>
                                            </header>
                                        </Column>                                        
                                    </columns>
                                    <items>
                                        <ColumnListItem id="_IDGenColumnListItem">
                                            <cells>
                                                <Text id="_IDGenText" text="{POINT}" />
                                                <Text id="_IDGenText1" text="{PTTXT}"/>
                                                <Input width="100%" id="read123" enabled="{= ${MRNGU} === '' ? false : true}" value="{EnterReading}"
                                                       maxLength="8" type="Number" liveChange="liveChangefn"/>                                                                                                                                                        
                                                <Select id="_IDSelect" enabled="{= ${NOCOD} === '' ? false : true}"

                                                </Select>
                                                <Text id="_IDGenText2" text="{RECDU}"/>
                                                <Text id="_IDGenText3" text="{READG}"/>
                                                <Text id="_IDGenText4" text="{KURZTEXT}"/>
                                            </cells>

                                    </items>
                                </Table>
                            </IconTabFilter>

Thanks,

Bharath

entry.title: Re: Modify the Financial Review booklet
entry.content:
Hi Eyal,
you find in the review booklet variables, which are on booklet level (in the filter bar) and more page specific variables are defined and changed on page level.
The FS Item hierarchy as well as the reporting item hierarchy can be changed on page level.
So when you run your booklet you have to open the side panel called page filters.
Here you can change the hierarchy ID as well as filter the hierarchy nodes you want to see on your page.
hope this helps?

Kind Regards Stefan

entry.title: So wenden Sie sich an das SAP Education - Learning-Hub-Team um Unterstützung zu erhalten
entry.content:
** This blog was in collaboration/translation with my German speaking colleague Rebecca Dolan

Benötigen Sie Unterstützung vom Learning Hub/Schulungsteam erhalten und wissen nicht, wo Sie anfangen sollen? Dann ist dieser Blog für Sie…  

Hier zeige ich Ihnen die Schritte, um das SAP Learning Support Center zu kontaktieren. Die Support-Mitarbeiter sind Montag bis Freitag, rund um die Uhr verfügbar, und die meisten Anfragen werden innerhalb von 24 Stunden beantwortet. 

* Klicken Sie hier, wenn Sie dies auf Englisch oder Spanisch lesen möchten

 

In der Support-Plattform finden Sie auch häufig gestellte Fragen zu folgenden Themen:  

  • Allgemeine Support-Themen  
  • Praxissysteme  
  • Themen von Schulungs-Reseller-Partnern  
  • Lern- und Zertifizierungsberatung   
  • Kursbuchung und Bezahlung   
  • Und Analyse-Dashboard 

 Anlegen eines Falls  

Wenn Sie feststellen, dass Sie auf der Supportplattform nicht die gewünschten Informationen finden, können Sie einen Fall anlegen, um Unterstützung mit Ihrer Anfrage zu erhalten.   

Dazu müssen Sie sich lediglich mit Ihrer Universal ID anmelden und ein Ticket erstellen. 

Auf der SAP-Learning-Hub-Website:  

  1. Klicken Sie auf das Fragezeichen (?) rechts oben auf der Seite.
  2. Auf der Help-Center-Seite wählen Sie dann auf die Registerkarte „Contact Us“, und klicken dann auf „Request Technical Assistance“. 
  3. Klicken Sie dann auf „Need More Help“. 

     

  4. Melden Sie sich mit Ihrer Universal ID an, und klicken Sie dann auf "Create Case". 

 Auf der SAP-Learning-Website:  

  1. Scrollen Sie nach unten zum Ende der Seite, und klicken Sie unter „Support and Contact“ auf „Get Support“. 
  2. Nun werden Sie auf die User-Support-Center Webseite weitergeleitet. Dort finden Sie die FAQs zur Selbsthilfe und detaillierte Leitfäden.  
  3. Wenn der erforderliche Support nicht in den FAQs gefunden wird, scrollen Sie nach unten, und klicken auf das „Need Help“ Symbol. Sie werden dann zur nächsten Seite weitergeleitet, um ein Ticket zu erstellen.  
  4. Klicken Sie "Create Case". 

     

Darüber hinaus können Sie den direkten Link zum User-Support-Center verwenden.  

Um den Status Ihres Tickets zu sehen, klicken Sie auf den Refresh-Button, um den Status ihres Tickets zu aktualisieren. 

Worin können Supportmitarbeiter Sie unterstützen?  

Das Schulungsteam kann Sie unterstützen, wenn Sie Informationen zu Kursen, Zertifizierungsinhalten und Learning Journeys erhalten möchten. Außerdem kann es Sie beraten, um zu verstehen, welche Schulungen erforderlich sind, um Ihre Ziele zu erreichen.  

Weitere Leistungen sind:   

  • Fragen zur Zertifizierung, einschließlich Ratschläge zur korrekten UID-Einrichtung und wie Sie auf Ihre Badges zugreifen können.   
  • Probleme im Zusammenhang mit Informations-, Liefer- und Nachsorgeleistungen von Unterrichtsräumen mit Kursleiter.  
  • Buchungen und abrechnungsbezogene Probleme werden in der Regel von einem anderen internen Team bearbeitet, aber das Learning-Team kann sich einbringen, um die Genehmigung für kostenlose Umbuchungen und ähnliche Szenarien zu erteilen, da diese häufig mit Kundenreklamationen usw. zusammenhängen.  
  • Visabriefe.  
  • Bereitstellen von Lieferanteninformationen auf Anforderung. 

Fordern Sie Unterstützung in einer anderen Sprache an.  

Wenn Sie möchten, dass das Schulungsteam Sie in Ihrer bevorzugten Sprache unterstützt, können Sie die Sprache auswählen, nachdem Sie die Beschreibung hinzugefügt haben. Sie müssen das Land auswählen, in dem Sie sich befinden, und zwischen Deutsch, Englisch, Spanisch, Französisch und Japanisch wählen. 

Zusätzlich empfehlen wir Ihnen den Blog  SAP Certifications and UID...all you need to know! Um mehr zum Them SAP-Zertifizierungen und UID zu erfahren. 

Der folgende Wissensdatenbankartikel kann auch hilfreich sein: 2404504 - Hilfe zum SAP Learning Hub oder SAP Training Center 

Danke fürs Lesen!

entry.title: 32-bit CR.NET support question
entry.content:
We are currently using the 32-bit CR .NET Runtime in our application. According to this SAP documentation,  https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354088411.html, the 32-bit CR .NET Runtime will be discontinued after December 2025. Could you clarify what "discontinued" means in this context? Will it still be available for use? Additionally, how will this affect   our application that have already been shipped to customers earlier and are still in use?

From the same page, it appears that the overall end of support for Crystal Reports is 2027. Do you have any recommendations for a migration path once Crystal Reports reaches its end of life?

entry.title: Cómo comunicarse con el equipo de SAP Education - Learning Hub para obtener ayuda
entry.content:
¿Está buscando obtener ayuda del SAP Education/Learning Hub y no sabe por dónde empezar? Entonces este blog es para usted... 

Aquí compartiré los pasos que debes seguir para interactuar con el área de soporte; los agentes están disponibles las 24 horas del día, de lunes a viernes, y la mayoría de las consultas se responden dentro de las 24 horas. 

* Haga click aqui si desea leer este blog en Alemán o en Inglés

En  la plataforma de soporte también podrás encontrar preguntas frecuentes relacionadas con: 

  • Temas de soporte general 
  • Sistemas de práctica 
  • Temas de capacitación para socios revendedores 
  • Asesoramiento sobre aprendizaje y certificación  
  • Reserva y pago de cursos  
  • Y panel de análisis 

Cómo crear un caso 

Si la información que está buscando no está en la plataforma, puede crear un caso para obtener ayuda sobre su consulta específica.  

Todo lo que necesita hacer es iniciar sesión con su Universal ID y crear un ticket. 

Desde el sitio SAP Learning Hub : 

  1. Debe hacer clic en el ícono del signo de interrogación (?) que se encuentra en la parte superior derecha de la página. 
  2. En la página del Centro de ayuda, haga clic en el botón “Contáctenos” y seleccione “Solicitar asistencia técnica”
     
  3. Haga clic en "¿Necesita más ayuda?" 
  4. Inicie sesión con su ID Universal y luego deberá hacer clic en "Crear caso" 

Desde el sitio de aprendizaje de SAP : 

  1. Desplácese hasta la parte inferior de la página y haga clic en “Obtener soporte”, que se encuentra en Soporte y contacto. 
  2. Será dirigido a la siguiente página, la página del Centro de soporte al usuario, donde podrá encontrar las preguntas frecuentes de autoayuda y guías detalladas. 
  3. Si no encuentra la ayuda que necesita en las preguntas frecuentes, desplácese hacia abajo y haga clic en el ícono "¿Necesita ayuda?" y será dirigido a la página siguiente para generar un ticket. 
  4. Haga clic en el botón “Crear caso”. 

Además, puede utilizar el enlace directo al Centro de soporte al usuario . 

Para ver la actualización de su ticket: haga clic en el botón Actualizar antes de encontrar la actualización de su caso/ticket. 

¿En qué pueden ayudarle los agentes de soporte? 

El Equipo de Aprendizaje puede ayudarle si desea obtener información sobre cursos, contenido de certificación y rutas de aprendizaje. También pueden asesorarle para que comprenda qué aprendizaje es necesario para lograr sus objetivos. 

Otros servicios incluyen:  

  • Preguntas de primer nivel sobre certificación, incluido asesoramiento sobre la configuración correcta del UID y cómo acceder a sus credenciales.  
  • Cuestiones relacionadas con la información, la prestación y el servicio posterior a las clases dirigidas por instructores. 
  • Los problemas relacionados con las reservas y la facturación generalmente los maneja otro equipo interno, pero el equipo de aprendizaje puede involucrarse para dar aprobación para cualquier nueva reserva gratuita y situaciones similares, ya que a menudo están relacionadas con quejas de los clientes, etc. 
  • Cartas de visa. 
  • Proporcionar información de proveedores a pedido. 

Solicitar soporte en un idioma diferente. 

Si desea que el equipo de aprendizaje lo ayude en su idioma preferido, puede seleccionar el idioma una vez que haya agregado la descripción, deberá elegir el país en el que se encuentra y seleccionar entre: Alemán, Inglés, Español, Francés y Japonés. 

También nos gustaría compartir este blog para que puedas aprender más sobre las certificaciones SAP y UID... ¡todo lo que necesitas saber! Y este KBA también puede ser útil:  2404504 - Cómo obtener ayuda con SAP Learning Hub o SAP Training Center 

¡Gracias por leer!

entry.title: Global benefits provisioning job didn't work for persons who is terminated and newly hired
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a scenario where employee is terminated and newly hired in a different country, so in this case person ID remain same but User ID changes however when I run the benefits master data changes provisioning job then the log shows "Processed 0 users" in Execution Manager Dashboard which means that the system is unable to recognise this user based on the person ID provided in the job parameters.

In a different scenario, when when we run the benefit master data changes Job for Global assignment person ID's, the system successfully processed the users to validate the eligibility criteria for benefit plan enrolment.

The common factor in both the scenarios is person ID remain same and user id changes. So what is the criteria in the first case why system does not process the person when terminated and newly hired.

Please let me know if anyone is experienced in Benefits module and came across this situation and how to fix it.

Thanks,

Thrilleswar.

entry.title: Re: Python samples in ASE did not work and fail with an I...
entry.content:
Hi,

This actually isn't a typo, but documentation that hasn't been updated for newer versions of the module.

I've created a CR or change request for these Python pages. I've found 3 pages so far.

The python modules are built with specific python versions.
Windows
%SYBASE%\%SYBASE_OCS%\python\pythonXX_64
All other platforms
$SYBASE/$SYBASE_OCS/python/pythonXX_64r

XX will be the python version that the module is built for in that directory.
So python 3.11 would use python311_64r

Hope that helps,
Ryan
